# UC-22.50.14 · Swiss nFADP Art.7 — privacy-by-design checkpoints in the SDLC

## Description

Measures what fraction of production-bound merges include a DPIA and a privacy-by-design review checkpoint.

## Value

nFADP Art.7 explicitly requires privacy-by-design; the UC ties the statutory duty to measurable SDLC KPIs.

## Implementation

(1) Ensure GitOps events carry reviewer_role, dpia_id, dpia_status; (2) schedule weekly; (3) publish per-team glass table. Indexes required: `index=gitops`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: SDLC / GitOps pipeline logs, code-review audit events, DPIA tracker..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ensure GitOps events carry reviewer_role, dpia_id, dpia_status; (2) schedule weekly; (3) publish per-team glass table.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=gitops earliest=-90d
| search event="merge" AND target_branch="main"
| eval dpia_completed=if(isnotnull(dpia_id) AND dpia_status="signed", 1, 0), pbd_review=if(reviewer_role="dpo" OR reviewer_role="privacy_engineer",1,0)
| stats sum(dpia_completed) AS dpias sum(pbd_review) AS pbd_reviews count AS merges BY repo, team
| eval dpia_rate=round(100*dpias/merges,1), pbd_rate=round(100*pbd_reviews/merges,1)
| where pbd_rate < 100 OR dpia_rate < 100
```

Understanding this SPL

**Swiss nFADP Art.7 — privacy-by-design checkpoints in the SDLC** — nFADP Art.7 explicitly requires privacy-by-design; the UC ties the statutory duty to measurable SDLC KPIs.

Documented **Data sources**: SDLC / GitOps pipeline logs, code-review audit events, DPIA tracker. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: gitops.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=gitops, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **dpia_completed** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by repo, team** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **dpia_rate** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where pbd_rate < 100 OR dpia_rate < 100` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the GRC system and the audit register, and with the crosswalk for the tier-2 framework clause, so monitoring evidence matches the mapped control.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner.

## SPL

```spl
index=gitops earliest=-90d
| search event="merge" AND target_branch="main"
| eval dpia_completed=if(isnotnull(dpia_id) AND dpia_status="signed", 1, 0), pbd_review=if(reviewer_role="dpo" OR reviewer_role="privacy_engineer",1,0)
| stats sum(dpia_completed) AS dpias sum(pbd_review) AS pbd_reviews count AS merges BY repo, team
| eval dpia_rate=round(100*dpias/merges,1), pbd_rate=round(100*pbd_reviews/merges,1)
| where pbd_rate < 100 OR dpia_rate < 100
```

## Visualization

Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner. Key fields: `dpia_completed`, `dpia_rate`, `dpias`, `merges`, `pbd_reviews`, `repo`.

## References

- [Swiss Federal Act on Data Protection (nFADP)](https://www.fedlex.admin.ch/eli/cc/1993/1945_1945_1945/en)
